# Candidate RS-MCA leaderboard rows from certificate scanner

Generated by `leaderboard_sweep.py`.  Rows marked `PROVED` use the direct moving-root tangent lower floor. Rows marked `DRAFT_PAPER_D_V5` use the Paper D v5 universal cap and must remain draft-theorem examples until human revision and peer review.

## Interpretation

- `gap eta` is `a/n - k/n`, i.e. distance below capacity. Smaller is closer to the Proximity Prize boundary.
- `score = 128 + log2(numerator/denominator)`, matching the frontier board scoring convention.
- Paper D rows report an effective numerator floor from `epsilon > (q-n)/(2kq)`: `floor((q-n)/(2k))+1`.
- Exact-prime prize rows use primes `q = 1 mod n` near `2^192`; they are field-instantiated candidates, not just bit-size placeholders.
